name: WP-W3-envelope-router: enforce envelope/router trust-boundary integrity fail-closed
description: W3 (envelope/router, deepest pre-tail WP on the critical path) — enforce the structured response envelope (llmResponseEnvelopeSchema, parse via parseEnvelope()) fail-closed; escape learner text before it reaches the system prompt; every envelope signal carries a server-side hard cap (MMT-ADR-0016/0014). Subsumes by construction F-133 (WI-549) and the inngest-authority group F-019/F-020/F-092 (WI-550), folded here as verification ACs — which is why this WP cannot close before W2 lands (it may still begin its W1-spine envelope work earlier).
acceptanceCriteria: Out-of-range envelope field hard-fails; no raw envelope or fail-open allowlist path; learner text escaped; regression ACs — the WP-W0-patch-api (WI-549) break-test for F-133 and the WP-W0-patch-inngest (WI-550) break-tests for F-019/F-020/F-092 still pass against the rebuilt model. Findings: F-025 (hard-fail on out-of-range field), F-131 (streamed vs persisted divergence), F-136 (raw-envelope leak on empty reply), F-137 (allowlist fail-open), F-141 (unescaped learner text into system prompt) (5 new). Source of truth: _wip/identity-foundation/2026-06-09-phase-o-master-plan.md §1 WP-W3-envelope-router (commit 23d0c01ad)
riskImpact: M / risky — the LLM trust boundary (prompt-injection + fail-open).
bodyText:
Bundle brief (WP-W3-envelope-router)
Transcribed from the ratified Phase-O master plan §1 → "WP-W3-envelope-router" @ 23d0c01ad — frozen source of truth. Children are absorbed provenance (ZDX-ADR-0001): WI-610/611.
Bundle rationale
All are envelope/router trust-boundary defects on the W1 spine (range hard-fail, stream/persist divergence, raw-envelope leak, allowlist fail-open, prompt-injection via learner text).
Strategy
Enforce the structured response envelope (llmResponseEnvelopeSchema, parse via parseEnvelope()) fail-closed; escape learner text before it reaches the system prompt; every envelope signal carries a server-side hard cap (MMT-ADR-0016/0014). Wire the W1 policy-engine scaffold into the LLM call path — including the documented NoEligibleModelError → CircuitOpenError mapping obligation (see PR #860 JSDoc, policy-engine/router.ts).
Scope
In: F-025 (hard-fail on out-of-range field), F-131 (streamed vs persisted divergence), F-136 (raw-envelope leak on empty reply), F-137 (allowlist fail-open), F-141 (unescaped learner text into system prompt). Out: the W0-patched router defect (subsumed) and inngest-authority (subsumed).
Findings table
Subsumes by construction: F-133 (policy-blocked fail-over, W0/WI-549); inngest-authority group F-019/F-020/F-092 (W0/WI-550) — folded as verification ACs.
Acceptance Criteria (bundle)
Out-of-range envelope field hard-fails; no raw envelope or fail-open allowlist path; learner text escaped.
Regression ACs: the WI-549 break-test for F-133 and the WI-550 break-tests for F-019/F-020/F-092 still pass against the rebuilt model.
Risk & blast radius
M / risky — the LLM trust boundary (prompt-injection + fail-open). Depends on WP-W1-spine (WI-571) + WP-W2-scope-rls (WI-574) + WP-W2-consent-deletion (WI-576) — the subsumed inngest-authority regression ACs verify against the rebuilt W2 tenancy/consent model, so this WP cannot CLOSE before W2 lands (envelope work may begin earlier).
